rationale: Webb's operationalization refers to reactive avoidance (stimulus-driven withdrawal) rather than deliberate planning.
Webb's operationalization refers to rumination (stimulus-driven fixation on emotional content) rather than deliberate attentional control.
The situation modification might require flexible integration of information to manage related elements such as people and resources.
The distraction redirects the attentional process and involves more than stimulus-driven responding.
The expressive suppression is the result of controlled processing.
The "other" is vague. I classified it as B under the assumption that it relates to bodily/action adjustment, but it could also fit the other two categories.
Reappraisal requires re-interpreting meaning and relating emotional information to make an adjustment.
Perspectives taking requires the person to increase understanding across multiple views and broaden recognition.
The "Other" can be vague to decide, but the change is commonly influenced by multiple factors.
The acceptance can be managed through rule-based processing and practice, regardless of whether it is effective.
